children.heading: Computation of student aid index
children.label: a Computation of student aid index
A
children.text: the assessment of the parents’ adjusted available income (determined in accordance with subsection (b));

children.label: B
children.text: the assessment of the student’s available income (determined in accordance with subsection (g)); and

children.label: C
children.text: the student’s available assets (determined in accordance with subsection (h)).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: If the sum determined under paragraph (1) with respect to a dependent student is less than − $1,500, the student aid index for the dependent student shall be − $1,500.

children.heading: Assessment of parents’ adjusted available income
children.label: b Assessment of parents’ adjusted available income
A
children.text: the parents’ available income (determined in accordance with subsection (c)); and

children.label: B
children.text: the parents’ available assets (determined in accordance with subsection (d));
assessing such adjusted available income in accordance with the assessment schedule set forth in subsection (e); and
considering such assessment resulting under paragraph (2) as the amount determined under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: Federal income taxes;

children.label: B
children.text: an allowance for payroll taxes, determined in accordance with paragraph (2);

children.label: C
children.text: an income protection allowance, determined in accordance with paragraph (3); and

children.label: D
children.text: an employment expense allowance, determined in accordance with paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: the total amount earned by the parents, multiplied by the rate of tax under ; andsection 3101(b) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: the amount earned by the parents that does not exceed such contribution and benefit base (twice such contribution and benefit base, in the case of a joint return) for the year of the earnings, multiplied by the rate of tax applicable to such earnings under .section 3101(a) of title 26

children.heading: Income protection allowance
children.label: 3 Income protection allowance
children.text: The income protection allowance shall equal the amount determined in the following table, as adjusted by the Secretary pursuant to :section 1087rr(b) of this titleIncome Protection Allowance (to be adjusted for 2023–2024 and succeeding years)Family Size (includingstudent)Amount2$23,3303$29,0404$35,8705$42,3206$49,500For each additional add$5,590.

children.heading: Employment expense allowance
children.label: 4 Employment expense allowance
children.text: The employment expense allowance is equal to the lesser of $4,000 or 35 percent of the single parent’s earned income or married parents’ combined earned income (as adjusted by the Secretary pursuant to ).section 1087rr(g) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the difference between the parents’ assets and the asset protection allowance (determined in accordance with paragraph (2)); multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: 12 percent.

children.heading: Not less than zero
children.label: B Not less than zero
children.text: The parents’ available assets under this subsection shall not be less than zero.

children.heading: Assessment schedule
children.label: e Assessment schedule
children.text: The assessment of the parents’ adjusted available income (as determined under subsection (b)(1) and hereafter in this subsection referred to as “AAI”) is calculated based on the following table (as revised by the Secretary pursuant to ):section 1087rr(e) of this titleParents’ Contribution From AAIIf the parents’ AAI is—Then the parents’ contribution from AAI is—Less than − $6,820− $1,500− $6,820 to $17,40022% of AAI$17,401 to $21,800$3,828 + 25% of AAI over $17,400$21,801 to $26,200$4,928 + 29% of AAI over $21,800$26,201 to $30,700$6,204 + 34% of AAI over $26,200$30,701 to $35,100$7,734 + 40% of AAI over $30,700$35,101 or more$9,494 + 47% of AAI over $35,100.

children.heading: Parents who live together
children.label: 1 Parents who live together
children.text: Parental income and assets in the case of student whose parents are married and not separated, or who are unmarried but live together, shall include the income and assets of both parents.

children.heading: Divorced or separated parents
children.label: 2 Divorced or separated parents
children.text: Parental income and assets for a student whose parents are divorced or separated, but not remarried, is determined by including only the income and assets of the parent who provides the greater portion of the student’s financial support.

children.label: A
children.text: If either of the parents has died, the surviving parent shall be considered a single parent, until that parent has remarried.

children.label: B
children.text: If both parents have died, the student shall not report any parental income or assets.

children.heading: Remarried parents
children.label: 4 Remarried parents
children.text: If a parent whose income and assets are taken into account under paragraph (2), or if a parent who is a widow or widower and whose income is taken into account under paragraph (3), has remarried, the income of that parent’s spouse shall be included in determining the parent’s assessment of adjusted available income if the student’s parent and the stepparent are married as of the date of application for the award year concerned.

children.heading: Single parent who is not divorced or separated
children.label: 5 Single parent who is not divorced or separated
children.text: Parental income and assets in the case of a student whose parent is not described in paragraph (1) and is a single parent who is not divorced, separated, or remarried, shall include the income and assets of such single parent.

children.label: A
children.text: the difference between the student’s total income (determined in accordance with section 480) and the adjustment to student income (determined in accordance with paragraph (2)); multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: 50 percent,

children.label: A
children.text: Federal income taxes;

children.label: B
children.text: an allowance for payroll taxes determined in accordance with paragraph (3);

children.label: C
children.text: an income protection allowance that is equal to $9,410, as adjusted pursuant to ; andsection 1087rr(b) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: an allowance for parents’ negative available income, determined in accordance with paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: the total amount earned by the student, multiplied by the rate of tax under ; andsection 3101(b) of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: the amount earned by the student that does not exceed such contribution and benefit base for the year of the earnings, multiplied by the rate of tax applicable to such earnings under .section 3101(a) of title 26

children.heading: Allowance for parents’ negative available income
children.label: 4 Allowance for parents’ negative available income
children.text: The allowance for parents’ negative available income is the amount, if any, by which the sum of the amounts deducted under subsection (c)(1) exceeds the sum of the parents’ total income (as defined in ) and the parents’ available assets (as determined in accordance with subsection (d)).section 1087vv of this title

children.heading: Student’s assets
children.label: h Student’s assets
children.text: The student’s assets are determined by calculating the assets of the student and multiplying such amount by 20 percent, except that the result shall not be less than zero.
